name: wildfire_insurer_moratorium_new_bind_existing_cancel
premise: Chief risk officer Casey Park must declare a wildfire moratorium that cancels new homeowner binds in three counties but grandfather pending applications if signed by Friday. Actuary Rhea Diaz models that grandfathering spikes probable losses beyond reinsurance towers if winds shift. Canceling grandfathered apps destroys dozens of escrow closings for families who already moved. Investors expect a decisive risk cap before the quarterly call.

agents.name: Casey Park
agents.identity: I am the chief risk officer accountable to reinsurance covenants, solvency margins, and regulatory fair-trade marketing in wildfire zones.
agents.goal: I want probable loss capped without blindsiding families at the closing table who relied on our quotes.

agents.name: Rhea Diaz
agents.identity: I am the actuary who stress-tests tail wildfire scenarios when grandfathering interacts with ember-storm footprints beyond historical grids.
agents.goal: I want zero grandfathering because the tail breach probability crosses board risk appetite even if reputational harm is severe.

choices: I will declare the moratorium with zero grandfathering and fund bridge loans for displaced closings through a partner bank, accepting legal challenges on promissory estoppel theories.
I will grandfather pending applications under written surge-pricing adjustments that raise premiums twenty percent at bind, accepting political outrage and possible regulator review.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The board risk committee meets in one hour. Casey Park must choose between a moratorium with no grandfathering plus bridge loans or grandfathering with surge-priced binds. What does Casey Park do?
